claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions. Remember, Child Benefit is assessed for each child individually.

**First question: Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how old is the child (or how old are the children)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 16 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask you a few more questions to determine eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in approved education or training, or has he recently left education or training?**

(Approved education or training typically includes things like A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or certain training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He recently left education, actually. He's just signed up with the careers service to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Since Alex recently left education, I need to check if he might still qualify under the extension period.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - since Alex has registered with the careers service, he may be eligible for a 20-week extension period after leaving education.

**When did Alex leave education? I need to know if it's been within the last 20 weeks.**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been about a couple of months since he left, so yes, still within that 20-week period.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex left education within the last 20 weeks and has registered with the careers service, he may still qualify during this extension period.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all right now. He's just focusing on figuring out his next steps.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not receiving any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't started an apprenticeship or anything like that yet.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. Now I need to ask about your own circumstances.

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Do you live with Alex, or do you contribute to his upkeep (such as paying for his care, food, clothes, housing, etc.)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, another parent or guardian)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital
- In local authority care
- Living with foster parents

Or is he living normally at home with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's living normally at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: **Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards his care.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why Alex qualifies:
- Alex is 16 years old and recently left education within the last 20 weeks
- He has registered with the careers service, which means he qualifies for the 20-week extension period
- He is not working 24 hours or more per week
- He is not receiving benefits in his own right
- He is not in an apprenticeship
- You live in the UK and Alex lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- The local council is not paying for his care

**Important things to note:**
- The 20-week extension will end either when the 20 weeks are up or when Alex turns 18, whichever comes first
- If Alex starts approved education or training (such as A-levels, T-levels, or NVQs up to level 3) before the extension ends, he may continue to qualify for Child Benefit until he turns 20, as long as he studies at least 12 hours per week
- If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, the High Income Child Benefit Charge may apply (this doesn't affect eligibility, but you may need to pay back some or all of the benefit through your tax return)

**Next steps:**
- You can apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate

Would you like any further information about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you so much! That's really helpful. I understand everything now and I'll get started with the application.
